name: HMS Conway Castle (FY 509)
events: 27 Dec 1942 After taking on board three ratings that had to come from Dundee, HrMs Dolfijn (Lt.Cdr. H.M.L.F.E. van Oostrom Soede, RNN), departed Falmouth around 0930 A/27 to continue her passage to Gibraltar. She was escorted out (until 1740 A/27) by HMS Lord Inchcape (T/Lt. A.C. Baxter, RNVR) and HMS Conway Castle (T/Lt. N.G. Nell, RNVR).

They were to pass through position 180° - Lizard Head - 3 miles, thence through 180° - Bishop Rock - 10 miles westwards until dusk, thence submarine to the westward and the trawlers returning to Falmouth.
HrMs Dolfijn was to pass through position 43°01'N, 13°50'E at 0001 A/2. (1)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
